what_input_feature: The profile colorspace signature field (FOURCC at input offsets 0x10-0x13) must equal the 4-byte literal '1CLR' (31 43 4c 52) so the switch over ProfileSpace dispatches to the case cmsSig1colorData arm; the common 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) FOURCC instead falls to case cmsSigRgbData.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving Seed 1 carries 31 43 4c 52 ('1CLR') at offsets 0x10-0x13, and the byte-diff shows the winning offsets 0x10-0x12 hold non-RGB values (e.g. 31/43/4c) while losers are uniformly R/G/B; value_profile's CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs whose signature operand moves progressively closer to the 1CLR constant, so the matching FOURCC is assembled and the true case-arm is taken (case cmsSig1colorData is hit only in W).
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds hold the fixed FOURCC 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13, which dispatches to case cmsSigRgbData; naive has only edge-count feedback and no comparison-distance gradient, so random havoc never assembles the exact 1CLR signature and the blocker case stays unreached (L: T=0 at line 827).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile augments naive's edge counters with CMP_MAP buckets keyed by operand-distance; the switch on the colorspace signature compiles to integer comparisons against the case constants, so value_profile receives a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient guiding the FOURCC field toward the cmsSig1colorData constant, whereas naive (random byte mutation, no CMP introspection) cannot. This is a CMP_MAP gradient effect, not input-to-state substitution (value_profile has no I2S mutator).

what_input_feature: A 4-byte profile-colorspace signature field at input offset 0x10-0x13 holding the exact FOURCC 'MCHC' (4d 43 48 43), which equals cmsSigMCHCData and selects the switch case at cmspcs.c:860.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry 'MCHC' (4d 43 48 43) at offsets 0x10-0x13, the exact FOURCC the switch case cmsSigMCHCData compares against, so _cmsLCMScolorSpace takes the true branch at line 860.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13, which matches case cmsSigRgbData (line 815) instead, so the switch never reaches the cmsSigMCHCData case and the true side stays blocked.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback: each integer/string CMP against the colorspace signature writes a matching-prefix / Hamming-distance bucket, so an input whose signature bytes get progressively closer to 'MCHC' is preserved as corpus and the fuzzer climbs the gradient to the exact 4-byte match. naive has only edge-counter feedback and no signal that distinguishes a near-match signature from any other wrong value, so it must hit the exact FOURCC by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte color-space signature at offset 0x10-0x13 must equal the FOURCC 'MCH2' (bytes 4D 43 48 32). cmsChannelsOfColorSpace switches on this 32-bit ColorSpace value; the cmsSigMCH2Data arm is reached only on an exact match, so the input needs all four bytes M,C,H,2 simultaneously at those offsets.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'MCH2' at offsets 0x10-0x13 (bytes 4D 43 48 32). The BYTE DIFF confirms W bytes are 4D/43/48/32 at 0x10/0x11/0x12/0x13 while concentrated, i.e. the winner inputs sit exactly on the magic FOURCC.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13 instead of 'MCH2', so the switch falls into the cmsSigRgbData/3-channel arms (the L-hit case lines 889-898) and never reaches case cmsSigMCH2Data.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP comparing the 32-bit ColorSpace signature against the cmsSigMCH2Data constant and writes a Hamming-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; inputs whose signature is progressively closer to 'MCH2' add new CMP_MAP buckets and are preserved, giving value_profile a gradient toward the 4-byte match that naive's blind havoc cannot synthesize. This is CMP_MAP gradient feedback, not input-to-state substitution (value_profile has no I2S mutator).

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace-signature field (offsets 0x10-0x13) must equal the literal 'MCH5' (4d 43 48 35), which decodes to cmsSigMCH5Data so the switch in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace selects the true (blocked) arm at line 905.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed carries bytes 4d 43 48 35 ('MCH5') at offsets 0x10-0x13 exactly where the loser seeds carry a different signature; this is the only constant the switch needs to land on case cmsSigMCH5Data and increment the branch hitcount to 1.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at offsets 0x10-0x13, decoding to cmsSigRgbData (a different switch arm at line 893), so they never reach case cmsSigMCH5Data and stay blocked across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer/string CMP for the colorspace signature and writes a matching-prefix/Hamming distance bucket into CMP_MAP; a mutation that gets the signature byte-closer to 'MCH5' than any seen pair adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that climbs to the exact 4-byte FOURCC. naive lacks CMP interception, so it must blind-guess all 4 bytes via havoc and never lands them.

what_input_feature: A hex character-reference construct that enters xmlParseCharRef's hex loop (lines 2327-2332) and then presents a byte that fails ALL three hex-digit range tests ([0-9], [a-f], [A-F]), so control reaches the else arm at line 2333 which calls xmlFatalErr(ctxt, XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF, NULL) and lands on case 256.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry char-ref/markup material (e.g. '(#PCD', '-UCS-4', ';link9href') and the per-role overlay marks the hex-digit range comparisons and the else->xmlFatalErr arm (lines 2327, 2329, 2331, 2333-2337) as [W] winner-only, and the blocker case 256 itself as [W]; the branch true side is taken 12 times for W but 0 times for L.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds are well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>' DOCTYPE/SYSTEM documents that never assemble a hex char-ref terminated by a non-hex byte; at line 256 the loser takes the false side 147 times and the true side 0 times, so the invalid-hex-charref arm is never reached.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback scores the chained hex-digit range comparisons (RAW vs '0'/'9'/'a'/'f'/'A'/'F') in xmlParseCharRef, preserving inputs that drive operands toward and then just past the hex-digit boundary; this gradient lets value_profile incrementally construct a &#x reference whose terminating byte fails the range tests, while naive's edge-only feedback gives no signal on how close a byte is to a hex digit and cannot stage that construction.

what_input_feature: A character-reference prefix where RAW=='&', NXT(1)=='#', and NXT(2)=='x' (the literal byte 0x78), i.e. the '&#x' hex-charref opener rather than the '&#' decimal form.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolved the true branch in all 10 trials; the per-role overlay shows the winner taking the hex path (lines 2318-2344 marked [W]) and hitting line 2317 true (T=12) while line 2349 (the decimal else-if) is never taken by W.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocked 9/10; its inputs reach RAW=='&' and NXT(1)=='#' (line 2316 L T=84) but the third byte is not 'x', so it falls through to the decimal '&#' else-if at line 2349 (L T=84) and stays on the false side of line 2317 (L T=0 F=84).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP for NXT(2)=='x' and writes a Hamming-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose third byte is closer to 0x78 adds a new bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that drives the byte toward 'x'. naive has no CMP introspection and only edge-counter feedback, so it can only land 'x' by lucky havoc byte mutation, explaining 1/10.

what_input_feature: A decimal character reference (`&#` then a long ASCII-digit run) where each digit satisfies the `(RAW >= '0') && (RAW <= '9')` range comparison, so the loop keeps advancing through enough digits to push count past 20.
why_winner_satisfies: The loop body compares each byte against the '0'..'9' range at L2359; value_profile's CMP_MAP records how close each byte is to those bounds, so inputs whose bytes approach valid digits are preserved and extended, lengthening the digit run. The value_profile pair resolves 9/10 with branch hitcount 8 versus the loser's 1.
why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser seeds reach the loop only 4 times (L2352 T=4) and average hitcount 1 on the branch; without CMP-distance feedback naive gets no gradient toward filling the run with in-range digit bytes, so the loop exits early via the invalid-char break before count exceeds 20.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds per-CMP CMP_MAP distance buckets on the digit-range comparisons at L2359, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient that rewards bytes closer to valid decimal digits. This drives progressively longer digit runs that eventually trip the count++>20 reset branch — a CMP_MAP gradient effect.

function: hb-buffer-verify.cc:buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break(hb_buffer_t*, hb_buffer_t*, hb_font_t*, hb_feature_t const*, unsigned int, char const* const*)
what_input_feature: A structurally valid sfnt/font input (consistent table header at offsets 0x00-0x0f, e.g. the '01 46 ... kern' shape) that shapes into multiple glyphs and sets HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK on at least one glyph, making info[end-(forward?0:1)].mask & HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK true at line 124.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner reaches line 124 with T=540 F=0 (the OR-arm true side fires), and value_profile's CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs whose CMP operands approach matching the font-table magic/length checks, steering the corpus toward valid sfnt headers that survive shaping; winning seeds carry the consistent '01 ... kern' header at offsets 0x00-0x0f.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 trials and at line 124 reaches only T=0 F=180 (mask bit never set); loser seeds are short/garbled (6-byte and 35-byte inputs with no coherent table header) so they never shape into a buffer whose glyphs carry the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK flag.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's per-CMP distance-bucket feedback (CMP_MAP gradient) gives partial credit for getting closer to satisfying the font-format integer comparisons, so it climbs toward valid headers that produce shaped buffers with UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyphs; naive has no such gradient and stalls on the all-or-nothing checks.

what_input_feature: Input bytes that decode (via the harfbuzz fuzzer harness) to a Sinhala-script buffer whose current glyph codepoint equals U+0D91 (little-endian bytes 91 0d ...), so the equality switch on buffer->cur().codepoint dispatches to case 0x0D91u.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile's CMP_MAP records how close each integer CMP came to matching, so the codepoint==0x0D91 comparison gets a distance-bucket gradient that preserves near-miss inputs and climbs toward the exact match; winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 91 0d codepoint bytes (e.g. value_profile seed 3 has 91 0d at offsets 0x08 and 0x0c).
why_loser_doesnt: naive only has bare edge-counter feedback; the 0x0D91 switch arm is one discrete value among many Sinhala cases, so naive must blindly hit the exact 16-bit codepoint with no proximity signal and stays blocked all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP per-(PC, operand-distance bucket) feedback gives a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient on the codepoint==0x0D91 integer comparison, rewarding near-miss codepoints and letting the fuzzer hill-climb to the exact value naive cannot find.

source_line: case OP_Concat: {           /* same as TK_CONCAT, in1, in2, out3 */
what_input_feature: A well-formed SQL statement whose expression tree contains a string concatenation, i.e. the literal '||' operator (bytes 7c 7c) between two text operands inside a SELECT, so the parser emits an OP_Concat opcode that the VDBE reaches at /src/sqlite3/bld/sqlite3.c:86705.
why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds are syntactically valid SELECT statements containing '||': seed 1 has 'SELECT 11-0133=1...3=1||3=13' with 7c 7c at offset 0x32, and seeds 2 and 3 carry 'SELECT'''L''''''' followed by '||' (7c 7c) at offsets 0x11-0x12; the BYTE DIFF confirms W concentrates on 7c(|)x2 at offsets 0x11 and 0x12 and on the SELECT keyword bytes 53(S)/45(E)/4c(L)/45(E)/43(C)/54(T) at offsets 0x00-0x05.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds are non-parsing byte garbage (e.g. 'T 1; c BINARY' or '``g..;......y' or high-byte blobs) with no SELECT keyword and no '||' operator at any offset, so they never compile to a program containing OP_Concat and OP_Concat stays at T=0 in the loser column.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket) over intercepted CMPs in the SQL tokenizer/parser; inputs that match more of the 'SELECT' keyword prefix or get a closer byte match on the '||' token produce a new closer CMP_MAP bucket and are kept as corpus, letting value_profile climb the gradient toward syntactically valid concatenation SQL that naive's plain edge feedback cannot assemble by luck.

what_input_feature: The SQL input must contain a dotted column/table reference (an ID '.' ID token sequence such as M.L), so the parser emits a TK_DOT expression node; the switch in resolveExprStep then takes the TK_DOT case arm at line 98355 and descends into the pExpr->op!=TK_ID else-block (lines 98366-98386).
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner seed begins 'SELECT M.L. .L.4' with literal '.' (0x2e) bytes separating identifier characters (M, L), forming the ID.ID dotted-reference structure that parses to a TK_DOT node; value_profile resolved 9/10 trials and was the only seed to take the true side (W=1 true at L98355).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds contain no dotted-reference structure: they are runs of '+' (0x2b), digits/commas ('SELECT ( 111111,'), or '~'/'<' fillers, so the parser never builds a TK_DOT node and the switch falls through to TK_ID / TK_VARIABLE / TK_LT arms instead; naive blocked 9/10 with 0 hits on the true side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted token/character comparisons (e.g. the byte comparison that classifies an expression operator as the '.' / TK_DOT token) move closer to matching, preserving partial-progress seeds that naive's pure edge-coverage discards; this Hamming/prefix-distance bucketing lets value_profile incrementally assemble the rare ID.ID dotted syntax that naive cannot stumble onto with havoc bytes alone.

what_input_feature: A SQL statement parsed into an expression tree whose code generation reaches line 103567 and supplies an integer-CMP operand that must equal/approach the constant on the true side; the winner builds this via an arithmetic expression with operators (*, <<, %) over multi-digit numeric literals.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winning seed is 'SELECT 1*$1<< 11119099%.19999666...' — a deeply nested arithmetic expression with operators and long numeric literals, exactly the integer-comparison-rich code path that CMP_MAP distance feedback can climb; the branch true side fires (W: T=1 F=6 at L103567) and avg hitcount on the branch is 22.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are either 'vacuum' / 'vacuum;;...' (a different statement type that never codes this expression comparison) or trivial 'SELECT 1;;+...' / 'SELECT?19CCCCCC...' forms lacking the arithmetic operand structure; the loser takes only the false side (L: T=0 F=892 at L103567) and never hits the branch (avg hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts integer CMPs and writes a (PC, distance-bucket) CMP_MAP entry; an input whose arithmetic operand lands closer to the true-side constant adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient toward the exact value naive must hit by blind luck.

what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose result columns include a qualified-wildcard reference (e.g. 'LLTL.*' — an identifier followed by '.' and '*'), so selectExpander reaches the true side of the wildcard/column-name expansion comparison at line 134117 rather than the degenerate false side.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT LLTL.*. .' — a SELECT with a qualified '.*' wildcard column reference; in the divergent-branch trace W takes the true side at the blocker (L134117 W: T=1 F=0) and progresses past it (W reaches L134120/L134132/L134154/L134252 with T=1), reflecting that the column-expansion path executed.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are degenerate result lists like 'SELECT ( 111111,' and 'SELECT+SE+...' / 'SELECT ~~~~~44<4' with no '<ident>.*' wildcard token; in the trace L reaches L134112 (T=223) and L134114 but takes the false side at the blocker (L134117 L: T=0 F=223), never satisfying the comparison the true branch needs.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance-bucket) for the integer/string CMPs inside selectExpander's name-resolution / wildcard match; as havoc mutants drift their column-reference bytes toward a matching prefix, each closer operand pair opens a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that climbs toward the exact comparison the true branch gates on. naive's plain edge-counter feedback gets no such intermediate reward and only flips the branch by luck.

what_input_feature: An input whose leading bytes spell the SELECT keyword followed by expression tokens (dotted-list / column-reference structure), reaching the deep expression-parse reduction in yy_reduce at 157646.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds begin with the literal bytes 53 45 4c 45 43 54 20 ('SELECT ') and continue with dotted-list expression tokens, so they drive resolveExprStep (15 fires in W, 0 in L) and hit the blocker true side 11 times.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser-blocking seeds do not start with SELECT (they begin with 'T ', backticks, 'E', ';'), never enter the expression-resolution path, and take the blocker false side 61/61.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance (matching-prefix length) for the tokenizer/keyword integer and string CMPs; an input one byte closer to spelling SELECT and the subsequent expression tokens adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, so VP ratchets bytes toward the valid SELECT-expression prefix that naive cannot assemble by blind havoc.
